(51) / GKZ7A0 and Fig. fig-4loop-4q) defines the U-type 4-loop vacuum integral with 7 propagators carrying momenta {q1, q1+q3+q4, q2, q2+q3+q4, q3+q4, q3, q4}. Decomposing into independent loop cycles, q1 lives only in propagators 1 and 2 (so they form a parallel pair between two vertices), q2 lives only in 3 and 4 (parallel pair), and the difference of the q3 and q4 cycles is supported on propagators 6 and 7 (parallel pair). The paper's Fig. 5 (fig-4loop-5q.pdf, Sec. 5.2 'Z-type', Eq. (66)/GKZ7B0) shows a 4-vertex 4-loop vacuum graph with TWO separate double edges and one diagonal. Reading off the seven propagators of Eq. (66) with momentum conservation at each vertex (A=upper-left, B=upper-right, C=lower-left, D=lower-right): m1 and m2 are two parallel propagators A-B; m3 is A-C; m4 is the Z-diagonal C-B; m5 is B-D; m6 and m7 are two parallel propagators C-D. All four vertices have valence >=3 (A:3, B:4, C:4, D:3).
